title: Tangentiality
summary: Proves that the kth ambient Laplacian power is tangential at weight k - n/2 and packages the resulting canonical GJMS family.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Proves that the kth ambient Laplacian power is tangential at weight k - n/2 and packages the resulting canonical GJMS family.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows GJMSoperators/Tangentiality.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Every Q-multiple vanishes modulo Q. This duplicate local lemma keeps this file independent of theorem names in Tangential.lean. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem vanishesModQ_Qmul_local (h : Function Conf) : CalConf.VanishesModQ (CalConf.Qmul h)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A local way to construct equality modulo Q. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,f,g\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\;h\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{VanishesModQ} (U \longmapsto f U - g U),\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{SameBoundaryValue} f g
correspondence.leanType: theorem sameBoundaryValue_of_difference_vanishes {f g : Function Conf} (h : CalConf.VanishesModQ (fun U => f U - g U)) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f g

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The key vanishing statement: If h has the critical error weight k - n / 2 - 2, then Δ̃^k(Qh) vanishes modulo Q. This is the precise algebraic reason why Δ̃^k descends to the boundary. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lapPow_Qmul_vanishes_at_criticalWeight (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) {h : Function Conf} (hh : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous (calculusInputWeight CalConf k - 2) h) : CalConf.VanishesModQ (CalConf.lapPow k (CalConf.Qmul h))

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: If two homogeneous functions of the GJMS input weight differ by a Q-multiple, then applying Δ̃^k preserves equality modulo Q. This is the direct mod Q form of tangentiality. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,H\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{AlgebraicIdentities},\;k\colon \mathbb{N},\;f,g\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\;_hf\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsXHomogeneous} (w_{\mathrm{in}} \operatorname{CalConf} k) f,\;_hg\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsXHomogeneous} (w_{\mathrm{in}} \operatorname{CalConf} k) g,\;hfg\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{SameBoundaryValueAtWeight} (w_{\mathrm{in}} \operatorname{CalConf} k) f g,\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{SameBoundaryValue} (\operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lapPow} k f) (\operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lapPow} k g)
correspondence.leanType: theorem lapPow_preserves_sameBoundaryValue_at_criticalWeight (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) {f g : Function Conf} (_hf : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous (calculusInputWeight CalConf k) f) (_hg : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous (calculusInputWeight CalConf k) g) (hfg : CalConf.SameBoundaryValueAtWeight (calculusInputWeight CalConf k) f g) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue (CalConf.lapPow k f) (CalConf.lapPow k g)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Main theorem: Δ̃^k is tangential at the GJMS critical input weight. In other words, Δ̃^k : E[k - n / 2] → E[-k - n / 2] is well-defined on boundary densities, provided the extra tangentiality identities are available. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lapPow_isTangentialAtCriticalWeight (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeight (ambientLaplacianPowerOperator CalConf k) (calculusInputWeight CalConf k)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The canonical ambient GJMS operator obtained from Δ̃^k, once the tangentiality identities are available. In explicit terms, for h of type cal conf.algebraic identities, and k in the natural numbers, its defining expression is ambient Laplacian Power Abstract Operator Cal Conf H k lap Pow is Tangential At Critical Weight Cal Conf H k.
correspondence.leanType: def canonicalAmbientGJMS (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) : AbstractOperator CalConf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, and k in the natural numbers, canonical Ambient GJMS Cal Conf H k.k equals k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMS_k (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) : (canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H k).k = k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, and k in the natural numbers, canonical Ambient GJMS Cal Conf H k.order equals 2 times k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMS_order (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) : (canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H k).order = 2 * k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, and k in the natural numbers, canonical Ambient GJMS Cal Conf H k.to Operator equals ambient Laplacian Power Operator Cal Conf k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMS_toOperator (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) : (canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H k).toOperator = ambientLaplacianPowerOperator CalConf k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Applying the canonical ambient GJMS operator is the same as applying lapPow k. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMS_toOperator_apply (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) (f : Function Conf) : (canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H k).toOperator f = CalConf.lapPow k f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The canonical ambient GJMS operator maps the GJMS input weight to the GJMS output weight. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMS_mapsWeight (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) : CalConf.MapsHomogeneousWeight (canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H k).toOperator (calculusInputWeight CalConf k) (calculusOutputWeight CalConf k)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The canonical ambient GJMS operator is tangential at the GJMS input weight. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMS_tangential (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeight (canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H k).toOperator (calculusInputWeight CalConf k)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The whole canonical GJMS family obtained from all powers of the ambient Laplacian. In explicit terms, for h of type cal conf.algebraic identities, its defining expression is operator : equals the function sending k to canonical Ambient GJMS Cal Conf H k operator k : equals by intro k rfl.
For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, and k in the natural numbers, canonical Ambient GJMSFamily Cal Conf H.get k equals canonical Ambient GJMS Cal Conf H k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMSFamily_get (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) : (canonicalAmbientGJMSFamily CalConf H).get k = canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, and k in the natural numbers, canonical Ambient GJMSFamily Cal Conf H.get k.order equals 2 times k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMSFamily_get_order (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) : ((canonicalAmbientGJMSFamily CalConf H).get k).order = 2 * k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The zeroth canonical ambient GJMS operator is represented by the identity. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMS_zero_toOperator_apply (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (f : Function Conf) : (canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H 0).toOperator f = f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The first nontrivial canonical ambient GJMS operator is represented by the ambient Laplacian. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMS_one_toOperator_apply (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (f : Function Conf) : (canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H 1).toOperator f = CalConf.lap f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The second nontrivial canonical ambient GJMS operator is represented by lapPow 2. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalAmbientGJMS_two_toOperator_apply (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (f : Function Conf) : (canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H 2).toOperator f = CalConf.lapPow 2 f

references.note: Original construction of conformally invariant powers of the Laplacian.

references.locator: Ambient Laplacian and homogeneous extension discussion
references.note: Ambient-metric background for the formal construction.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
